title: Detection of a pair density wave state in UTe2
classification_notes: GPT error: Expecting value: line 209 column 19 (char 11050)

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports scanning tunnelling microscopy/spectroscopy evidence for a pair density wave (PDW) state in the heavy-fermion superconductor UTe2. Using both normal-metal and superconducting STM tips, the authors map spatial modulations of the superconducting gap and find three incommensurate PDW wavevectors that match previously reported charge-density-wave (CDW) wavevectors, with an approximately π phase shift between CDW and PDW images. The work argues that UTe2 hosts an unprecedented spin-triplet PDW intertwined with its CDW.
measurement_types: SIS tunnelling spectroscopy with superconducting tip
Fourier analysis / lock-in filtered real-space maps

figures.description: Schematic crystal structure of UTe2 oriented to the a, b, c axes and the (0–11) cleave plane.
Schematic of elemental identities and atomic sites in the (0–11) termination layer.
Schematic Fermi surface in the (kx, ky) plane at kz = 0 and an illustrative superconducting order parameter magnitude Δ(k).
Typical STM topographic image T(r) of the UTe2 (0–11) surface measured with a superconducting tip at 280 mK.
Fourier transform T(q) of the topography showing surface reciprocal-lattice points.
Topographic STM image T(r) of the (0–11) surface at 4.2 K with reciprocal-lattice points marked in the Fourier inset.
Differential conductance map g(r, 10 mV) showing normal-state electronic modulations.
Fourier transform g(q, 10 mV) showing three incommensurate CDW peaks Q1, Q2, Q3.
Inverse-Fourier-filtered real-space CDW image gQ(r, 10 mV) using only Q1, Q2, Q3.
Normal-tip NIS tunnelling spectrum dI/dV showing weak superconducting coherence peaks near ±ΔUTe2.
Superconducting-tip SIS spectrum dI/dV showing enhanced coherence peaks near ±(Δtip + Δsample) and subgap features.
SIS tunnelling topograph T(r) over the same field of view used for gap mapping.
Color map of SIS spectra along a line cut showing spatial modulation of positive and negative coherence-peak energies E+(r) and E−(r).
Spatial map of the positive-bias superconducting gap Δ+(r).
Spatial map of the negative-bias superconducting gap Δ−(r).
Map of the superconducting gap modulation δΔ(r) extracted from SIS data.
Fourier transform δΔ(q) showing three PDW peaks P1, P2, P3 and lattice peaks.
Inverse-Fourier-filtered real-space PDW image from δΔ(q) at P1, P2, P3.
Inverse-Fourier-filtered real-space CDW image gQ(r, −9 mV) from the same field of view.
Comparison/statistical relationship showing the PDW and CDW images are approximately negative images of each other.
Histogram/statistics of relative phase differences δφi between CDW and PDW at Qi:Pi, centered near π.
Line cut comparing coterminous CDW and PDW signals along a trajectory.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements are STM/STS datasets collected on cleaved UTe2 (0–11) surfaces, including topographic scans T(r), differential conductance maps g(r, V), and tunnelling spectra dI/dV for both normal-metal tips (NIS) and superconducting Nb tips (SIS). Measurements were taken at 4.2 K in the normal state and at 280 mK in the superconducting state, with additional 1.5 K spectra used to determine the Nb tip gap. The paper also mentions subgap SIS spectra and line-scan spectral maps along specific trajectories.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that any methods, additional references, Nature Portfolio reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information, acknowledgements, peer review information, author contributions, competing interests, and statements of data and code availability are available via the article DOI page. The provided text does not include the actual data availability wording, repository link, or file list.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as STM topographs, differential conductance maps, and tunnelling spectra acquired on UTe2 surfaces. The text does not specify file formats, repository names, or whether raw scan files are deposited, but it does describe the measurement conditions, bias ranges, temperatures, and field-of-view registration procedures.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Fourier transforms T(q), g(q, V), and δΔ(q); inverse-Fourier-filtered real-space images gQ(r, V) and PDW maps; gap maps Δ+(r), Δ−(r), and δΔ(r); line cuts; cross-correlation analyses; and fitted peak positions from second-order polynomial fits. The Methods also describe lock-in filtered CDW amplitudes and drift-corrected/registered images using the Lawler–Fujita algorithm and shear correction.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly presented in the main text and Extended Data as image maps, spectra, Fourier-space maps, filtered reconstructions, and statistical comparisons. The paper explicitly refers to Extended Data figures and to source data availability on the article page, but the provided text does not enumerate source-data files or their formats.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts or notebooks are mentioned in the provided text. The Methods describe algorithms and fitting procedures (lock-in filtering, Fourier filtering, Lawler–Fujita drift correction, polynomial peak fitting), but no code repository or script files are specified here.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is strongly image-centric and multi-stage: raw STM/STS measurements are transformed into processed gap maps and Fourier-filtered real-space reconstructions. The paper distinguishes normal-state CDW imaging from superconducting-state PDW imaging, often on the same field of view after drift correction and rigid registration. The main quantitative outputs are wavevectors Q1,2,3 / P1,2,3, gap modulations on the order of 10 μeV, and phase relationships near π. The text suggests that source data and possibly extended data are available, but the exact repository structure is not given in the excerpt.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The study reports direct STM measurements: topography T(r), differential conductance g(r, V), and dI/dV spectra collected at 4.2 K, 280 mK, and 1.5 K on UTe2.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper explicitly references 'source data' in the online content statement and presents many figure-derived maps and Fourier-filtered reconstructions that are typical source-data outputs.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “Any methods, additional references, Nature Portfolio reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information ... and statements of data and code availability are available at https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-023-05919-7.”
priority_modalities: Fourier-space maps g(q, V) and δΔ(q)
